item.title: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
item.description: Binance Holdings Ltd. is making preparations to stop providing services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Says Inflation Is in the Pipeline Despite Iran Deal
item.description: The European Central Bank must be prepared for inflation that’s been unleashed by the conflict in the Middle East but is yet to be felt, according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

item.title: Race to Turn AI Compute Into a Commodity Spurs New Crypto Boom
item.description: Ethan Vera thought Luxor Technology’s future was helping Bitcoin miners buy and sell mining hardware.

item.title: SpaceX Pounces on $60 Billion Cursor Takeover Days After IPO
item.description: Technically, SpaceX had 30 days following its record-shattering public debut to decide on a $60 billion takeover of the AI coding startup Cursor. In the end, all it took was two trading days.

item.title: World Bank Initiative Brings Electricity to 50 Million Africans
item.description: A World Bank initiative to provide electricity to 300 million Africans by 2030 has reached nearly a fifth of its goal, two years after its formation.

item.title: CSN Cement Sale Faces Questions as Holcim Resists Huaxin Bid
item.description: Huaxin Cement Co.’s bid for the cement unit of Brazil’s Cia. Siderurgia Nacional is facing resistance from one of the Chinese company’s key shareholders, Holcim Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: UzNIF to Start Preparing IPO of Uzbekistan Airways
item.description: Uzbekistan’s National Investment Fund, or UzNIF, will start preparations for an initial public offering of Uzbekistan Airways as the fund moves to the next stage of its capital-attraction strategy.

item.title: Former De Beers CEO Penny Is Said to Lead Race for Diamond Giant
item.description: A consortium led by De Beers’s former boss Gareth Penny is leading the race to buy the world’s biggest diamond miner after the Iran war hampered other bidders, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: The Executive Coach Showing Sam Altman How to Lead With ‘Emotional Fluidity’
item.description: Joe Hudson is sure the winners of the impending tech revolution will be human.

item.title: Portobello Delays Sale of Legal-Tech Firm on AI Disruption Angst
item.description: Private equity firm Portobello Capital is delaying its sale of Spanish legal-tech firm Legálitas due to concerns by prospective investors that artificial intelligence will disrupt the sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: World Cup Exposes Growing Rift Over Prediction Markets
item.description: This year’s World Cup is the first since prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket exploded to popularity as a new way to bet on sports.

item.title: Poland Pursues Taiwan Investments to End Role as Assembly Hub
item.description: Poland is betting on a flood of investments and technology transfers from Taiwanese companies to reengineer its $1 trillion economy.

item.title: Microsoft’s Latest Surface PCs Offer Better Graphics, Faster AI
item.description: Microsoft Corp. introduced updated Surface Pro and Surface Laptop devices, refreshing both products with faster graphics and new chips from Qualcomm Inc. to more effectively court creative professionals and artificial intelligence super users.

item.title: US Housing Starts Drop to the Weakest Pace Since 2020
item.description: New residential construction in the US slowed to the weakest pace in six years, driven by a steep decline in apartment projects.

item.title: Brent Oil Falls Below $80 With US-Iran Deal Set to Add Supply
item.description: Oil fell below $80 a barrel for the first time since early March as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply.

item.title: UAE’s Adnoc Hires Oil Veteran to Lead Trading Unit’s Expansion
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co has hired commodities veteran Benoit Roulon to head up its trading arm, part of the long-term expansion plan at the Middle Eastern producer which quit OPEC last month.

item.title: Poland Floats ‘Copper Valley’ Idea as Lumina Metals Surges on Warsaw Bourse Debut
item.description: Poland aims to join the ranks of the world’s top copper producers as new mines planned by Canada’s Lumina Metals Corp. in the eastern European country are poised to more than double output from state-controlled giant KGHM Polska Miedz SA.

item.title: ECB’s Escriva Says Energy Disruption Will Persist Despite Deal
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Jose Luis Escriva warned that complications over energy supplies are likely to persist, despite an agreement to re-open the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Russia Ships Oil at Near-Record Pace as Kyiv Pummels Refineries
item.description: Shipments at year-to-date high as more processing plants are hit by Ukraine’s long-range drones

item.title: Man Group Sees ‘Bubble Risks’ as AI Bond Sales Break Records
item.description: Man Group Plc says “bubble risks” are mounting as bond sales boom in the race to build out artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Databricks Sales Double for Product That Competes With Snowflake
item.description: Databricks Inc., a privately held software maker that competes with Snowflake Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google, said its data warehousing business has more than doubled in size over the last year, reaching a $1.5 billion annual run rate.

item.title: Lunchboxes Signal Inflation Pain for Brazil White-Collar Workers
item.description: Brazil’s lunch rush is moving from restaurants to office microwaves.

item.title: Adani Ports Partners With Kaleris for AI Terminal Software
item.description: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s port unit is teaming up with US-based Kaleris to deploy an AI-driven software to operate the company’s 15 container terminals across nine ports.

item.title: UK to Reduce Military Operations Without Defense Funding Boost
item.description: The UK will be forced to “dial back” operations, exercises and training for its armed forces, Britain’s top uniformed military official said, underscoring the fallout from the budget battle that prompted the defense secretary to quit last week.

item.title: US Futures Waver as Oil Drops on Iran Hope; SpaceX Extends Gains
item.description: Equity futures were slighly higher on Tuesday as rising confidence about a US-Iran agreement sent oil prices lower. SpaceX is set for a more than 50% jump in in first three sessions if premarket gains are sustained.

item.title: UK Regulator Weighs Cutting Data Center Power Use at Peak Times
item.description: The UK’s energy regulator is weighing whether future data centers should be required to reduce power consumption during periods of system stress, part of a broader effort to accommodate a surge in electricity demand from artificial intelligence and cloud computing.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 16, 2026
item.description: US equity-index futures are higher after a three-day rally in stocks. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are up 0.1% at 7:40 a.m. in New York.

item.title: China’s Two-Speed Growth Model: Temporary Imbalance or Geostrategic Design?
item.description: The country’s external sector is booming at the same time domestic demand stalls.

item.title: Iran War Has Irrevocably Changed the Middle Eastern Oil Trade
item.description: Buyers of crude from the Persian Gulf will want to make sure they are better prepared to cope with any future disruptions.

item.title: DE Shaw Moves to Close $5 Billion Lithic Fund to New Money
item.description: D.E. Shaw & Co. is preparing to close another of its strategies to new money, adding to a wave of hedge funds spurning cash just as investor interest in the industry picks up.
